## Test Scenarios
1. File system errors:

   - Disk full simulation
   - Permission denied
   - Symlink handling

   - Multiple caam instances

3. Invalid data handling:
   - Corrupted profile files
   - Invalid JSON in auth files
   - Missing required fields
   - Version mismatch

4. Recovery workflows:
   - Recover from interrupted backup
   - Recover from interrupted restore
   - Clean up partial state

## Requirements
- Test actual error conditions (not mocked)

- Verify cleanup on errors

 {"id":"caam-5ed","title":"Cooldown tracking after limit hits","description":"Track when accounts hit limits and enforce cooldown periods.

## The Problem
If account A hits a limit and user immediately switches to account B, then back to A an hour later, this pattern is suspicious. Real users don't hit limits multiple times per day on the same account.

## Solution
Track limit events and enforce cooldowns:
1. User marks limit hit: `caam limit-hit claude` (or auto-detect in future)
2. System records: profile X hit limit at time T
3. Cooldown calculated: can't reuse until T + cooldown_hours
4. Activation blocked/warned if profile is in cooldown

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE limit_events (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  provider TEXT NOT NULL,
  profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  hit_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
  cooldown_until DATETIME NOT NULL,
  notes TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_limit_events_provider_profile ON limit_events(provider, profile_name);
CREATE INDEX idx_limit_events_cooldown ON limit_events(cooldown_until);
```

## Commands
```bash
# Mark current profile as hitting limit
caam limit-hit claude
# Output: Recorded limit hit for claude/work-account. Cooldown until 20:32.

# Mark specific profile
caam limit-hit codex backup-account

# Check cooldown status
caam cooldown status
# Output:
# claude/work-account: in cooldown (2h 15m remaining)
# codex/backup-account: ready
# codex/main: in cooldown (45m remaining)

# Clear cooldown manually (for testing)
caam cooldown clear claude work-account
```

## Activation Integration
When user tries to activate a profile in cooldown:
```
$ caam activate claude work-account

Warning: claude/work-account is in cooldown
  Limit hit: 2 hours ago
  Cooldown remaining: 4 hours
  
Use --force to activate anyway, or try one of:
  caam activate claude personal-account
  caam activate claude --next  (auto-select best available)

Proceed anyway? [y/N]
```

## Auto-detection (Future Enhancement)
Could potentially detect limit errors in `caam exec` output:
- Codex: 'You've hit your usage limit'
- Claude: rate limit errors
- Gemini: quota exceeded

This would be a separate enhancement task.

## Files
- internal/db/limit_events.go: DB operations for limit tracking
- internal/db/limit_events_test.go: Tests
- internal/stealth/cooldown.go: Cooldown logic
- cmd/caam/cmd/limit.go: limit-hit and cooldown commands
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Integration","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:47:26.364033647-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T00:11:02.980029392-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T00:11:02.980029392-05:00","close_reason":"Cooldown tracking implemented (DB + CLI + activate warning/--force + tests).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-5ed","depends_on_id":"caam-bgz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:47:38.495473582-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"caam-5ed","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix dependency and add --all flag:

## Purpose
Track when accounts hit rate limits and enforce cooldown periods before reuse.
This prevents the suspicious pattern of: Account A hits limit -> immediately switch to Account B.

## DEPENDENCY FIX
This feature does NOT depend on caam-108 (switch delay system).

Rationale: Cooldowns and delays are INDEPENDENT features:
- Delays: Wait N seconds before ANY switch (reduces automation fingerprint)
- Cooldowns: Wait N minutes before reusing a SPECIFIC account that hit a limit

You might want cooldowns without delays, or delays without cooldowns.
The only shared dependency is caam-bgz (config schema) since both need config.

## Implementation

### Tracking
When user reports a limit hit:
```bash
caam cooldown set claude/work --minutes=60
```

This stores:
- Provider/profile that hit the limit
- Timestamp of limit hit
- Cooldown duration

### Enforcement
On `caam activate`:
1. Check if target profile has active cooldown
2. If yes: show warning with time remaining
3. User can override with `--force` or wait

### Clearing
```bash
caam cooldown clear claude/work     # Clear specific
caam cooldown clear --all           # Clear ALL cooldowns (added!)
caam cooldown list                  # Show active cooldowns
```

## Config
```yaml
stealth:
  cooldown:
    enabled: false
    default_minutes: 60      # Default cooldown duration
    track_limit_hits: true   # Auto-track when limits detected
```

## Files
- internal/db/cooldown.go: Cooldown storage and queries
- internal/db/cooldown_test.go: Tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/cooldown.go: CLI subcommands (set, clear, list)
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add cooldown check before activation

## Success Criteria
- Can manually set cooldowns on profiles
- Activation warns when profile is in cooldown
- Can override with --force
- Can clear individual or ALL cooldowns

 {"id":"caam-5fk","title":"Auto-backup original state on first activate","description":"Automatically preserve the user's pre-caam authentication state.

## The Problem
User runs `caam activate claude foo` without backing up first → original auth is overwritten and LOST FOREVER. This is the highest-risk safety gap.

## Solution
On ANY activate command:
1. Check if auth files exist for the tool
2. Check if they match any existing vault profile (using hash comparison)
3. If auth exists AND no match found → this is 'virgin' state
4. Auto-backup to `_original_<tool>` profile
5. Print: 'Backed up original claude auth to _original_claude'
6. NEVER overwrite existing _original profile (it's a one-time safety net)

## Key Design Decisions
- Only happens ONCE per tool (when _original doesn't exist)
- Silent if _original already exists (idempotent)
- Uses protected profile naming (_original prefix)
- Stores metadata: {type: 'system', created_by: 'first-activate', original_paths: [...]}

## Implementation
1. Add Vault.HasOriginalBackup(tool) method
2. Add Vault.BackupOriginal(fileSet) method
3. Modify activate command to call BackupOriginal before restore
4. Add integration test: fresh system → activate → verify _original created

## Files
- internal/authfile/authfile.go: New methods
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add auto-backup logic
- internal/authfile/authfile_test.go: Unit tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate_test.go: Integration test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:21.406320231-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:07:00.583111467-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:07:00.583111467-05:00","close_reason":"Auto-backup original auth on first activate: added Vault.BackupOriginal/HasOriginalBackup and activate pre-restore safety. _original is protected/immutable; meta.json includes type=system, created_by=first-activate, original_paths; tests added.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-5fk","depends_on_id":"caam-nh8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:32.52430969-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"caam-5fk","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix profile naming:

## Implementation

On FIRST-EVER `caam activate` (any provider):
1. Check if `_original` profile exists for this provider
2. If NOT: automatically backup current auth state as `_original`
3. Proceed with normal activation

## Profile Naming

Profile name: `_original` (not `_original_<tool>`)

Rationale: The `_` prefix already marks it as protected/system-managed. Adding the tool name is redundant because:
- Profiles are already namespaced by provider (claude, codex, etc.)
- The vault structure is: `~/.caam/vault/<provider>/<profile_name>/`
- So claude's original is at: `~/.caam/vault/claude/_original/`
- And codex's original is at: `~/.caam/vault/codex/_original/`

No collision possible. Simpler naming = easier to remember and type.

## Protected Profile Behavior

Profiles starting with `_` are protected:
- Cannot be deleted via `caam delete`
- Cannot be overwritten via `caam save`
- Hidden from normal `caam list` (shown with `--all` or `-a`)
- Shown in distinct color/style when displayed

## Files
- internal/vault/vault.go: Add IsProtectedProfile() helper
- internal/vault/vault.go: Modify Save() to check for protected prefix
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add first-run backup logic
- cmd/caam/cmd/list.go: Filter protected by default, add --all flag

## Success Criteria
- First activate ALWAYS creates _original backup
- Protected profiles cannot be modified by user
- Clear visual distinction in listings

Added unit tests + activate integration test (codex).","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:06:48Z"}]}
-{"id":"caam-683","title":"Implement SSH connectivity and transport layer","description":"# Task: Implement SSH connectivity and transport layer

## Context
The sync feature uses SSH for secure transport between machines. This task implements the SSH connection handling and file transfer capabilities with a focus on "just works" UX while maintaining reasonable security.

## Implementation

### Location
`internal/sync/ssh.go` (new file)

### SSHClient Type
```go
type SSHClient struct {
    machine     *Machine
    client      *ssh.Client
    sftp        *sftp.Client
    connected   bool
    lastConnAt  time.Time
}

func NewSSHClient(m *Machine) *SSHClient
func (c *SSHClient) Connect(opts ConnectOptions) error
func (c *SSHClient) Disconnect() error
func (c *SSHClient) IsConnected() bool
func (c *SSHClient) TestConnection() (*ConnectivityResult, error)

type ConnectOptions struct {
    Timeout       time.Duration // Default: 10s
    UseAgent      bool          // Default: true (try ssh-agent first)
}
```

### File Operations
```go
// Read a file from remote machine
func (c *SSHClient) ReadFile(remotePath string) ([]byte, error)

// Write a file to remote machine (atomic via temp file + rename)
func (c *SSHClient) WriteFile(remotePath string, data []byte, mode os.FileMode) error

// Check if file exists on remote
func (c *SSHClient) FileExists(remotePath string) (bool, error)

// Get file modification time
func (c *SSHClient) FileModTime(remotePath string) (time.Time, error)

// List files in directory
func (c *SSHClient) ListDir(remotePath string) ([]os.FileInfo, error)

// Create directory (with parents)
func (c *SSHClient) MkdirAll(remotePath string) error

// Remove file
func (c *SSHClient) Remove(remotePath string) error
```

### Batch Operations
```go
// Transfer multiple files efficiently (single SFTP session)
func (c *SSHClient) BatchRead(paths []string) (map[string][]byte, error)
func (c *SSHClient) BatchWrite(files map[string][]byte, mode os.FileMode) error
```

## SSH Authentication

### Priority Order
1. **ssh-agent** (if available and has matching key)
2. **Key file** (from machine.SSHKeyPath)
3. **Default keys** (~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_rsa)

```go
func (c *SSHClient) getAuthMethods() ([]ssh.AuthMethod, error) {
    var methods []ssh.AuthMethod
    
    // 1. Try ssh-agent first
    if agent, err := getSSHAgent(); err == nil {
        methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeysCallback(agent.Signers))
    }
    
    // 2. Add key file if specified
    if c.machine.SSHKeyPath != "" {
        if signer, err := loadSSHKey(c.machine.SSHKeyPath); err == nil {
            methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeys(signer))
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Try default keys
    for _, keyPath := range defaultKeyPaths() {
        if signer, err := loadSSHKey(keyPath); err == nil {
            methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeys(signer))
        }
    }
    
    return methods, nil
}
```

### SSH Key Loading
```go
func loadSSHKey(keyPath string) (ssh.Signer, error) {
    keyPath = expandPath(keyPath) // ~ to home
    data, err := os.ReadFile(keyPath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    signer, err := ssh.ParsePrivateKey(data)
    if err != nil {
        // Check if passphrase protected
        if _, ok := err.(*ssh.PassphraseMissingError); ok {
            // Try ssh-agent or return helpful error
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("key %s is passphrase-protected; use ssh-agent", keyPath)
        }
        return nil, err
    }
    return signer, nil
}
```

## Host Key Verification ("Just Works" Approach)

**Philosophy**: Trust on first use (TOFU), like regular SSH.

```go
func (c *SSHClient) hostKeyCallback() ssh.HostKeyCallback {
    knownHostsPath := filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".ssh", "known_hosts")
    
    // Try to use existing known_hosts
    hostKeyCallback, err := knownhosts.New(knownHostsPath)
    if err == nil {
        // Wrap to auto-add unknown hosts
        return autoAddHostKeyCallback(hostKeyCallback, knownHostsPath)
    }
    
    // No known_hosts file - create one and auto-add
    return autoAddHostKeyCallback(nil, knownHostsPath)
}

// Auto-add unknown hosts on first connection (TOFU)
func autoAddHostKeyCallback(existing ssh.HostKeyCallback, knownHostsPath string) ssh.HostKeyCallback {
    return func(hostname string, remote net.Addr, key ssh.PublicKey) error {
        // First check if it's already known
        if existing != nil {
            err := existing(hostname, remote, key)
            if err == nil {
                return nil // Known and matches
            }
            
            // Check if it's a mismatch (security issue) vs just unknown
            var keyErr *knownhosts.KeyError
            if errors.As(err, &keyErr) && len(keyErr.Want) > 0 {
                // Key CHANGED - this is a security concern
                return fmt.Errorf("host key changed for %s - possible MITM attack", hostname)
            }
        }
        
        // Unknown host - auto-add with notice
        fmt.Printf("ℹ️  Adding %s to known hosts\n", hostname)
        addToKnownHosts(knownHostsPath, hostname, key)
        return nil
    }
}

func addToKnownHosts(path, hostname string, key ssh.PublicKey) error {
    // Ensure directory exists
    os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0700)
    
    // Append to known_hosts
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0600)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    
    line := knownhosts.Line([]string{hostname}, key)
    _, err = fmt.Fprintln(f, line)
    return err
}
```

**Security notes**:
- Unknown hosts are auto-added on first connection (TOFU)
- If a known host's key CHANGES, we reject (possible MITM)
- This matches how regular SSH works with StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new

## SFTP Implementation

Use `github.com/pkg/sftp` for reliable file transfer:

```go
func (c *SSHClient) ensureSFTP() error {
    if c.sftp != nil {
        return nil
    }
    sftp, err := sftp.NewClient(c.client)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("SFTP initialization failed: %w", err)
    }
    c.sftp = sftp
    return nil
}

func (c *SSHClient) ReadFile(remotePath string) ([]byte, error) {
    if err := c.ensureSFTP(); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    f, err := c.sftp.Open(remotePath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    
    return io.ReadAll(f)
}

func (c *SSHClient) WriteFile(remotePath string, data []byte, mode os.FileMode) error {
    if err := c.ensureSFTP(); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Atomic write: temp file + rename
    dir := filepath.Dir(remotePath)
    tmpPath := filepath.Join(dir, ".caam_tmp_"+randomString(8))
    
    f, err := c.sftp.Create(tmpPath)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
        f.Close()
        c.sftp.Remove(tmpPath)
        return err
    }
    f.Close()
    
    if err := c.sftp.Chmod(tmpPath, mode); err != nil {
        c.sftp.Remove(tmpPath)
        return err
    }
    
    return c.sftp.Rename(tmpPath, remotePath)
}
```

### Connection Pooling
```go
type ConnectionPool struct {
    clients map[string]*SSHClient
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    opts    ConnectOptions
}

func NewConnectionPool(opts ConnectOptions) *ConnectionPool
func (p *ConnectionPool) Get(machine *Machine) (*SSHClient, error)
func (p *ConnectionPool) Release(machineID string)
func (p *ConnectionPool) CloseAll()
```

### Error Handling
```go
type SSHError struct {
    Machine    *Machine
    Operation  string
    Underlying error
}

func (e *SSHError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("SSH error on %s during %s: %v", 
        e.Machine.Name, e.Operation, e.Underlying)
}

func (e *SSHError) IsTimeout() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsAuthFailure() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsNetworkError() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsHostKeyMismatch() bool
```

## Connectivity Test
```go
type ConnectivityResult struct {
    Machine      *Machine
    Success      bool
    Latency      time.Duration
    SSHVersion   string
    SFTPWorks    bool
    CAAMFound    bool           // caam data directory exists
    CAAMVersion  string         // caam version on remote (if detectable)
    ProfileCount int            // number of profiles on remote
    Error        error
    ErrorType    string         // "auth", "network", "hostkey", "sftp", etc.
}

func TestMachineConnectivity(m *Machine, opts ConnectOptions) *ConnectivityResult
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH key loading works (with ssh-agent priority)
- [ ] Passphrase-protected keys detected with helpful error
- [ ] Unknown hosts auto-added on first connect (TOFU)
- [ ] Changed host keys rejected (security)
- [ ] Connection establishment works
- [ ] Connection timeout handling
- [ ] File read/write via SFTP
- [ ] Atomic writes (temp + rename)
- [ ] Directory operations work
- [ ] Batch operations for efficiency
- [ ] Connection pooling implemented
- [ ] Error types properly categorized
- [ ] Connectivity test function returns detailed results
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked SSH

## Dependencies
- Go package: `golang.org/x/crypto/ssh`
- Go package: `github.com/pkg/sftp`
- Go package: `github.com/skeema/knownhosts`
- Go package: `golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/agent` (for ssh-agent)

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/ssh.go`
- `internal/sync/ssh_test.go`
- `internal/sync/connpool.go`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)

## Context
The sync feature uses SSH for secure transport between machines. This task implements the SSH connection handling and file transfer capabilities with a focus on "just works" UX while maintaining reasonable security.

## Implementation

### Location
`internal/sync/ssh.go` (new file)

### SSHClient Type
```go
type SSHClient struct {
    machine     *Machine
    client      *ssh.Client
```

### File Operations
```go

func (c *SSHClient) FileModTime(remotePath string) (time.Time, error)

func (c *SSHClient) ListDir(remotePath string) ([]os.FileInfo, error)

// Create directory (with parents)
func (c *SSHClient) MkdirAll(remotePath string) error

func (c *SSHClient) Remove(remotePath string) error
```

```go
// Transfer multiple files efficiently (single SFTP session)
func (c *SSHClient) BatchRead(paths []string) (map[string][]byte, error)
func (c *SSHClient) BatchWrite(files map[string][]byte, mode os.FileMode) error
```

## SSH Authentication

### Priority Order
1. **ssh-agent** (if available and has matching key)
2. **Key file** (from machine.SSHKeyPath)
3. **Default keys** (~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_rsa)

```go
func (c *SSHClient) getAuthMethods() ([]ssh.AuthMethod, error) {
    var methods []ssh.AuthMethod
    
    // 1. Try ssh-agent first
    if agent, err := getSSHAgent(); err == nil {
        methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeysCallback(agent.Signers))
    }
    
    // 2. Add key file if specified
    if c.machine.SSHKeyPath != "" {
        if signer, err := loadSSHKey(c.machine.SSHKeyPath); err == nil {
            methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeys(signer))
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Try default keys
    for _, keyPath := range defaultKeyPaths() {
        if signer, err := loadSSHKey(keyPath); err == nil {
            methods = append(methods, ssh.PublicKeys(signer))
        }
    }
    
    return methods, nil
}
```

### SSH Key Loading
```go
func loadSSHKey(keyPath string) (ssh.Signer, error) {
    keyPath = expandPath(keyPath) // ~ to home
    data, err := os.ReadFile(keyPath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    signer, err := ssh.ParsePrivateKey(data)
    if err != nil {
        // Check if passphrase protected
        if _, ok := err.(*ssh.PassphraseMissingError); ok {
            // Try ssh-agent or return helpful error
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("key %s is passphrase-protected; use ssh-agent", keyPath)
        }
        return nil, err
    }
    return signer, nil
}
```

## Host Key Verification ("Just Works" Approach)

**Philosophy**: Trust on first use (TOFU), like regular SSH.

```go
func (c *SSHClient) hostKeyCallback() ssh.HostKeyCallback {
    knownHostsPath := filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".ssh", "known_hosts")
    
    // Try to use existing known_hosts
    hostKeyCallback, err := knownhosts.New(knownHostsPath)
    if err == nil {
        // Wrap to auto-add unknown hosts
        return autoAddHostKeyCallback(hostKeyCallback, knownHostsPath)
    }
    
    // No known_hosts file - create one and auto-add
    return autoAddHostKeyCallback(nil, knownHostsPath)
}

// Auto-add unknown hosts on first connection (TOFU)
func autoAddHostKeyCallback(existing ssh.HostKeyCallback, knownHostsPath string) ssh.HostKeyCallback {
    return func(hostname string, remote net.Addr, key ssh.PublicKey) error {
        // First check if it's already known
        if existing != nil {
            err := existing(hostname, remote, key)
            if err == nil {
                return nil // Known and matches
            }
            
            // Check if it's a mismatch (security issue) vs just unknown
            var keyErr *knownhosts.KeyError
            if errors.As(err, &keyErr) && len(keyErr.Want) > 0 {
                // Key CHANGED - this is a security concern
                return fmt.Errorf("host key changed for %s - possible MITM attack", hostname)
            }
        }
        
        // Unknown host - auto-add with notice
        fmt.Printf("ℹ️  Adding %s to known hosts\n", hostname)
        addToKnownHosts(knownHostsPath, hostname, key)
        return nil
    }
}

func addToKnownHosts(path, hostname string, key ssh.PublicKey) error {
    // Ensure directory exists
    os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0700)
    
    // Append to known_hosts
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0600)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    
    line := knownhosts.Line([]string{hostname}, key)
    _, err = fmt.Fprintln(f, line)
    return err
}
```

**Security notes**:
- Unknown hosts are auto-added on first connection (TOFU)
- If a known host's key CHANGES, we reject (possible MITM)
- This matches how regular SSH works with StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new

## SFTP Implementation

Use `github.com/pkg/sftp` for reliable file transfer:

```go
func (c *SSHClient) ensureSFTP() error {
    if c.sftp != nil {
        return nil
    }
    sftp, err := sftp.NewClient(c.client)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("SFTP initialization failed: %w", err)
    }
    c.sftp = sftp
    return nil
}

func (c *SSHClient) ReadFile(remotePath string) ([]byte, error) {
    if err := c.ensureSFTP(); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    f, err := c.sftp.Open(remotePath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    
    return io.ReadAll(f)
}

func (c *SSHClient) WriteFile(remotePath string, data []byte, mode os.FileMode) error {
    if err := c.ensureSFTP(); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Atomic write: temp file + rename
    dir := filepath.Dir(remotePath)
    tmpPath := filepath.Join(dir, ".caam_tmp_"+randomString(8))
    
    f, err := c.sftp.Create(tmpPath)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
        f.Close()
        c.sftp.Remove(tmpPath)
        return err
    }
    f.Close()
    
    if err := c.sftp.Chmod(tmpPath, mode); err != nil {
        c.sftp.Remove(tmpPath)
        return err
    }
    
    return c.sftp.Rename(tmpPath, remotePath)
}
```

### Connection Pooling
```go
type ConnectionPool struct {
    clients map[string]*SSHClient
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    opts    ConnectOptions
}

func NewConnectionPool(opts ConnectOptions) *ConnectionPool
func (p *ConnectionPool) Get(machine *Machine) (*SSHClient, error)
func (p *ConnectionPool) Release(machineID string)
func (p *ConnectionPool) CloseAll()
```

### Error Handling
```go
type SSHError struct {
    Machine    *Machine
    Operation  string
    Underlying error
}

func (e *SSHError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("SSH error on %s during %s: %v", 
        e.Machine.Name, e.Operation, e.Underlying)
}

func (e *SSHError) IsTimeout() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsAuthFailure() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsNetworkError() bool
func (e *SSHError) IsHostKeyMismatch() bool
```


 {"id":"caam-6gi","title":"Penalty tracking with decay mechanism","description":"# Penalty Tracking with Decay

## Purpose
Implement the penalty decay mechanism from codex-pool to temporarily penalize problematic profiles while allowing natural recovery.

## Background (from codex-pool)
```go
func decayPenaltyLocked(a *Account, now time.Time) {
    if a.LastPenalty.IsZero() {
        a.LastPenalty = now
        return
    }
    if now.Sub(a.LastPenalty) < 5*time.Minute {
        return
    }
    // decay 20% every 5 minutes
    a.Penalty *= 0.8
    if a.Penalty < 0.01 {
        a.Penalty = 0
    }
    a.LastPenalty = now
}
```

## Why Decay?
- **Without decay**: One bad error = permanent penalty = profile never used
- **With decay**: Errors hurt temporarily, but profiles recover naturally
- **Result**: System is self-healing, no manual intervention needed

## Technical Requirements

### Penalty Events
| Event | Penalty Added |
|-------|---------------|
| Auth error (401/403) | +1.0 |
| Rate limit (429) | +0.5 |
| Server error (5xx) | +0.3 |
| Network timeout | +0.2 |
| Other error | +0.1 |

### Decay Algorithm
```go
const (
    DecayInterval = 5 * time.Minute
    DecayRate     = 0.8  // 20% decay
    MinPenalty    = 0.01 // Below this, reset to 0
)

func (h *ProfileHealth) DecayPenalty(now time.Time) {
    if h.PenaltyUpdatedAt.IsZero() {
        h.PenaltyUpdatedAt = now
        return
    }
    
    elapsed := now.Sub(h.PenaltyUpdatedAt)
    intervals := int(elapsed / DecayInterval)
    
    if intervals > 0 {
        for i := 0; i < intervals; i++ {
            h.Penalty *= DecayRate
        }
        if h.Penalty < MinPenalty {
            h.Penalty = 0
        }
        h.PenaltyUpdatedAt = now
    }
}

func (h *ProfileHealth) AddPenalty(amount float64, now time.Time) {
    h.DecayPenalty(now)  // Apply decay first
    h.Penalty += amount
    h.PenaltyUpdatedAt = now
}
```

### Error-to-Penalty Mapping
```go
func PenaltyForError(err error) float64 {
    // Check error type
    if isAuthError(err) {
        return 1.0
    }
    if isRateLimitError(err) {
        return 0.5
    }
    if isServerError(err) {
        return 0.3
    }
    if isTimeoutError(err) {
        return 0.2
    }
    return 0.1
}
```

## Decay Visualization
```
Time    Penalty   Status
0min    1.0       🔴 Critical
5min    0.8       🔴 Critical
10min   0.64      🟡 Warning
15min   0.51      🟡 Warning
20min   0.41      🟡 Warning
25min   0.33      🟢 Healthy
...
60min   0.01      🟢 Healthy (reset to 0)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DecayPenalty applies decay based on elapsed time
- [ ] AddPenalty applies decay then adds new penalty
- [ ] PenaltyForError maps errors to penalty amounts
- [ ] Penalty resets to 0 when below MinPenalty
- [ ] Unit tests with time mocking
- [ ] Integration with health status calculation

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/penalty.go` (new)
- `internal/health/penalty_test.go` (new)

 {"id":"caam-6gj","title":"Health metadata storage and persistence","description":"# Health Metadata Storage

## Purpose
Create the storage layer for profile health data including token expiry, error counts, penalties, and plan types.

## Technical Requirements

### File Location
`~/.caam/health.json`

### Data Structure
```go
type HealthStore struct {
    Profiles map[string]*ProfileHealth `json:"profiles"` // key: "provider/name"
    Version  int                       `json:"version"`
}

type ProfileHealth struct {
    TokenExpiresAt   time.Time `json:"token_expires_at,omitempty"`
    LastError        time.Time `json:"last_error,omitempty"`
    ErrorCount1h     int       `json:"error_count_1h"`
    Penalty          float64   `json:"penalty"`
    PenaltyUpdatedAt time.Time `json:"penalty_updated_at,omitempty"`
    PlanType         string    `json:"plan_type,omitempty"` // "free", "pro", "enterprise"
    LastChecked      time.Time `json:"last_checked,omitempty"`
}
```

### API Interface
```go
type HealthStorage interface {
    Load() (*HealthStore, error)
    Save(store *HealthStore) error
    GetProfile(provider, name string) (*ProfileHealth, error)
    UpdateProfile(provider, name string, health *ProfileHealth) error
    RecordError(provider, name string) error
    ClearErrors(provider, name string) error
}
```

### Implementation Notes
- Use atomic file writes (temp file + rename)
- Handle missing file gracefully (return empty store)
- Validate JSON structure on load
- Support concurrent access (file locking or mutex)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HealthStore struct defined in `internal/health/storage.go`
- [ ] Load/Save with atomic writes
- [ ] Profile-specific getters and setters
- [ ] Error recording increments error_count_1h
- [ ] Unit tests with temp directory
- [ ] Handles corrupted JSON gracefully

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/storage.go` (new)
- `internal/health/storage_test.go` (new)

 {"id":"caam-77v","title":"Implement 'doctor' command for diagnostics","description":"## Task
Add 'caam doctor' command to diagnose setup issues.

## Checks to Perform
1. **CLI binaries**: Are codex, claude, gemini installed and in PATH?
2. **Data directories**: Do ~/.local/share/caam/{vault,profiles} exist with correct permissions?
3. **Config**: Is config.json valid?
4. **Profiles**: Are all profiles valid? Any broken symlinks?
5. **Locks**: Any stale lock files?
6. **Auth files**: For each provider, do auth files exist in expected locations?

## Output Format
```
caam doctor

Checking CLI tools...
  ✓ codex found at /usr/local/bin/codex (v1.2.3)
  ✓ claude found at /usr/local/bin/claude (v2.0.1)
  ✗ gemini not found in PATH

Checking data directories...
  ✓ ~/.local/share/caam/vault exists (mode 0700)
  ✓ ~/.local/share/caam/profiles exists (mode 0700)

Checking profiles...
  ✓ codex/work-1: OK
  ⚠ codex/old-profile: Stale lock file (PID 12345 not running)
  ✗ claude/broken: Missing home directory

Checking auth files...
  ✓ codex: ~/.codex/auth.json exists
  ✓ claude: ~/.claude.json exists
  ✗ gemini: ~/.gemini/settings.json missing
```

## Flags
- --fix: Attempt to fix issues (create dirs, clean stale locks)
- --json: Machine-readable output

- Clear pass/fail/warning output
- --fix can resolve common issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:40.095003307-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:32:36.994420886-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:32:36.994420886-05:00","close_reason":"Doctor command implemented with all checks: CLI tools, data directories, config, profiles, stale locks, auth files. -{"id":"caam-7vg","title":"Implement TUI backup action","description":"# Task: Implement TUI backup action

## Context
Wire up the backup functionality to the TUI using the new text input dialog component.

## Implementation

### Trigger
- Press 'b' or 'B' when viewing a provider's profile list
- Only available when auth files exist for that provider

### Flow
1. User presses 'b' on a provider
2. Check if auth files exist for provider
   - If not: show error message "No auth files found for <provider>"
   - If yes: continue
3. Open text input dialog
   - Title: "Backup Current Auth"
   - Prompt: "Enter profile name (e.g., email address):"
   - Placeholder: "user@example.com"
4. User enters profile name, presses Enter
5. Validate input:
   - Not empty
   - No forbidden characters (/, \, etc.)
   - Warn if profile already exists (overwrite?)
6. Call vault.Backup(provider, profileName)
7. Handle result:
   - Success: show message "Backed up <provider> auth to '<profile>'"
   - Error: show error message
8. Refresh profile list

### Overwrite Confirmation
If profile already exists, show confirmation dialog:
- "Profile '<name>' already exists. Overwrite? (y/n)"
- Or use the dialog with Yes/No buttons

### Key Bindings
- Add 'b' to the help text
- Document in keyboard shortcuts section

## Code Changes

### internal/tui/update.go
```go
case "b", "B":
    if m.canBackup() {
        return m.showBackupDialog()
    }
```

### internal/tui/model.go
Add method:
```go
func (m Model) showBackupDialog() (Model, tea.Cmd) {
    // Open dialog for backup
}

func (m Model) executeBackup(profileName string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        err := m.vault.Backup(m.selectedProvider, profileName)
        // Return result message
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'b' key triggers backup dialog when on provider
- [ ] Dialog prompts for profile name
- [ ] Validation prevents empty/invalid names
- [ ] Existing profile triggers overwrite confirmation
- [ ] Successful backup shows confirmation message
- [ ] Failed backup shows error message
- [ ] Profile list refreshes after backup
- [ ] Help text updated with new keybinding

 {"id":"caam-82c","title":"Add force-unlock command","description":"## Task
Add 'caam unlock' command to forcibly remove locks.

## Usage
```bash
caam unlock codex work-1          # Unlock specific profile
caam unlock --stale               # Clean all stale locks
caam unlock codex work-1 --force  # Force unlock even if process running
```

## Safety
- Default: Only unlock if process not running
- --force: Unlock even if process appears running (dangerous)
- Warn before force unlock
- Consider sending SIGTERM to process before unlocking

## Implementation
- Check if lock is stale (PID not running)
- If stale, just remove lock file
- If --force, warn and remove anyway
- If not stale and no --force, error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can unlock stale locks
- Force requires explicit flag
- Warning before dangerous operations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.864750655-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam profile unlock command with stale lock detection and --force flag","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.879518279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.880805974-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-8y9","title":"Implement CLI sync commands","description":"# Task: Implement CLI sync commands

## Context
Implement the CLI commands for managing the sync pool and triggering synchronization.

## Commands

### `caam sync`
Sync now with all machines in pool.

```bash
caam sync [flags]

Flags:
  --machine NAME    Sync only with specific machine
  --provider PROV   Sync only specific provider
  --profile PROF    Sync only specific profile
  --dry-run         Show what would sync without doing it
  --force           Force sync even if recently synced
  --json            Output results as JSON
```

Output:
```
Syncing with 3 machines...

  work-laptop (192.168.1.100):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed (local fresher)
    ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com: pulled (remote fresher)
    ✓ codex/work@company.com: up to date
    
  home-desktop (10.0.0.50):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed
    ✓ gemini/personal@gmail.com: pulled
    
  cloud-vm (34.123.45.67):
    ✗ Connection failed: timeout

Sync complete: 4 pushed, 2 pulled, 1 up to date, 1 error
```

### `caam sync init` ⭐ NEW
Interactive first-time setup wizard:

```bash
caam sync init [flags]

Flags:
  --discover    Auto-discover from SSH config
  --csv         Create CSV template only
```

Flow:
```
$ caam sync init

Welcome to CAAM Sync Setup!

This will help you set up syncing between your machines.

Step 1: Create your sync pool

  Discovered hosts from ~/.ssh/config:
    [1] work-laptop (192.168.1.100)
    [2] home-desktop (10.0.0.50)
    [3] cloud-vm (34.123.45.67)
    [a] Add all discovered hosts
    [m] Manually add a machine
    [s] Skip for now

  Choice: a

Step 2: Test connectivity

  Testing work-laptop... ✓ OK (23ms)
  Testing home-desktop... ✓ OK (45ms)  
  Testing cloud-vm... ✗ Failed: connection refused

  1 machine failed. Continue anyway? (Y/n): y

Step 3: Enable auto-sync?

  Auto-sync automatically pushes fresh tokens when you backup or refresh.
  
  Enable auto-sync? (y/N): y

Setup complete!
  Machines in pool: 3 (2 online, 1 offline)
  Auto-sync: enabled

Run 'caam sync' to sync now, or it will happen automatically.
```

### `caam sync status`
Show sync pool status and last sync times.

```bash
caam sync status [flags]

Flags:
  --json    Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Status

Local Machine: jeffs-macbook
Auto-sync: enabled
Last full sync: 2025-12-19 14:29:00

Machines in pool:
  NAME           ADDRESS          STATUS    LAST SYNC
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    🟢 online  5 min ago
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        🟢 online  5 min ago
  cloud-vm       34.123.45.67     🔴 offline 2 hours ago (timeout)

Profiles tracked: 8
  claude: 5 profiles
  codex: 2 profiles
  gemini: 1 profile

Queue: 0 pending | History: 127 entries
```

### `caam sync add`
Add a machine to the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync add <name> <address> [flags]

Flags:
  --key PATH        Path to SSH private key (default: auto-detect)
  --user NAME       SSH username (default: current user)
  --remote-path P   Path to caam data on remote (default: same as local)
  --test            Test connectivity after adding
  --no-test         Skip connectivity test
```

Examples:
```bash
# Basic add (auto-detects SSH key)
caam sync add work-laptop 192.168.1.100

# With specific key and test
caam sync add cloud-vm 34.123.45.67 --key ~/.ssh/cloud_key --test

# With port and user in address
caam sync add dev-server jeff@dev.example.com:2222
```

### `caam sync remove`
Remove a machine from the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync remove <name> [flags]

Flags:
  --force    Skip confirmation
```

### `caam sync test`
Test connectivity to one or all machines.

```bash
caam sync test [name] [flags]

Flags:
  --all     Test all machines in pool
```

Output for single machine:
```
Testing connection to work-laptop (192.168.1.100)...
  SSH connection: ✓ OK (latency: 23ms)
  SFTP subsystem: ✓ OK
  CAAM data path: ✓ Found (~/.local/share/caam)
  CAAM version: v1.2.3 (compatible)
  Profiles found: 5
Connection successful!
```

Output for --all:
```
Testing all machines...
  work-laptop:   ✓ OK (23ms, 5 profiles)
  home-desktop:  ✓ OK (45ms, 3 profiles)
  cloud-vm:      ✗ Failed: connection refused

2/3 machines online
```

### `caam sync enable` / `caam sync disable`
Enable or disable auto-sync.

```bash
caam sync enable     # Enable auto-sync after backup/refresh
caam sync disable    # Disable auto-sync
```

### `caam sync log`
Show sync history.

```bash
caam sync log [flags]

Flags:
  --limit N     Show last N entries (default: 20)
  --machine M   Filter by machine
  --provider P  Filter by provider
  --errors      Show only errors
  --json        Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync History (last 20 entries)

TIME                 MACHINE        PROFILE                  ACTION   STATUS
2025-12-19 14:29:00  work-laptop    claude/alice@gmail.com   push     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:55  work-laptop    codex/work@company.com   pull     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:50  home-desktop   claude/bob@gmail.com     push     ✓
2025-12-19 12:15:30  cloud-vm       *                        sync     ✗ timeout
...
```

### `caam sync discover`
Discover machines from SSH config.

```bash
caam sync discover [flags]

Flags:
  --add       Add discovered machines to pool
  --test      Test connectivity to discovered machines
```

Output:
```
Discovered machines from ~/.ssh/config:

  NAME           ADDRESS          KEY                   STATUS
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    ~/.ssh/work_key      not in pool
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        ~/.ssh/id_ed25519    not in pool
  github         github.com       (skipped: git host)

Add discovered machines to sync pool? (y/N): y

Added 2 machines. Run 'caam sync test --all' to verify connectivity.
```

### `caam sync edit`
Open sync config (CSV file) in editor.

```bash
caam sync edit
```

Opens `~/.caam/sync_machines.csv` in $EDITOR (or sensible default).

### `caam sync queue`
Manage the retry queue.

```bash
caam sync queue [flags]

Flags:
  --clear     Clear all pending retries
  --process   Process pending retries now
  --json      Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Queue: 2 pending

  PROFILE                  MACHINE       ATTEMPTS   LAST ERROR
  claude/alice@gmail.com   cloud-vm      2          connection refused
  codex/work@company.com   cloud-vm      1          timeout

Use 'caam sync queue --process' to retry now.
Use 'caam sync queue --clear' to clear the queue.
```

## Implementation

### Location
`cmd/caam/cmd/sync.go` (new file)

### Structure
```go
var syncCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "sync",
    Short: "Sync vault across machines",
    Long:  `Synchronize auth profiles with other machines in your sync pool.`,
    RunE:  runSync,
}

var syncInitCmd = &cobra.Command{...}     // First-time setup wizard
var syncStatusCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncAddCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncRemoveCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncTestCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEnableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDisableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncLogCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDiscoverCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEditCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncQueueCmd = &cobra.Command{...}

func init() {
    rootCmd.AddCommand(syncCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncInitCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncStatusCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncAddCmd)
    // etc...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam sync` syncs with all machines
- [ ] `caam sync --machine` syncs with specific machine
- [ ] `caam sync --dry-run` shows preview
- [ ] `caam sync init` runs first-time setup wizard
- [ ] `caam sync status` shows pool status
- [ ] `caam sync add` adds machine to pool
- [ ] `caam sync remove` removes machine from pool
- [ ] `caam sync test` tests connectivity (single and all)
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` toggles auto-sync
- [ ] `caam sync log` shows history with filters
- [ ] `caam sync discover` finds machines from SSH config
- [ ] `caam sync edit` opens CSV in editor
- [ ] `caam sync queue` manages retry queue
- [ ] All commands have help text
- [ ] --json flag works for scriptability
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] Works gracefully when no machines configured

## Files to Create
- `cmd/caam/cmd/sync.go`
- `cmd/caam/cmd/sync_test.go`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Depends on: caam-npy (sync algorithm)

   - Backup/activate/status cycle
   - Profile isolation
   - Lock management
   - CLI command integration
   
3. **Test Infrastructure**
   - Detailed logging for debugging
   - Test fixtures and helpers
   - CI integration

## Philosophy
- NO mocks/fakes - test real behavior
- Tests should be runnable in isolation
- Clear failure messages with context
